key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 forms a denser, more protective oxide barrier than detonation-sprayed coating, giving better resistance to molten solar-salt corrosion at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For solar thermal energy storage containers, HVAF T-400 coatings appear more suitable than DS when long-term protection against nitrate-salt attack and spallation is required.
method: Deposited Tribaloy-400 on ASTM A516 steel using HVAF and detonation spraying.
Exposed coated samples to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten salt at 500 °C for up to 1000 h.
Compared phase formation, elemental mapping, SEM microstructure, and mass-gain/corrosion behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF maintained a uniform Cr–Mo–Si–O-rich surface with minimal salt penetration, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation over 1000 hours.
watch_item: Results are tied to a specific salt composition and test temperature, so performance may differ under other CSP operating conditions or thermal cycling.
paper_title: Hot Corrosion Behavior of Thermal Sprayed Using HVAF and DS Techniques with Tribaloy-400 Coatings for Solar Energy Storage Container
